rules.lessonHeading: Sanitize user-provided text before persisting to files
rules.message: Sanitize ANSI escape sequences (e.g., using 'strip-ansi') before writing user-provided text to .md or .log files to prevent terminal injection vulnerabilities.

rules.lessonHeading: Git diff headers wrap file paths containing spaces
rules.message: Git diff headers wrap paths with spaces in double quotes (e.g., +++ "b/path"). Ensure parsing logic accounts for optional quotes before the 'a/' or 'b/' prefix.

rules.lessonHeading: Synchronous execSync with piped stdio can cause the parent
rules.message: Synchronous execSync with piped stdio (the default) can cause the parent process to hang or abort silently. Use asynchronous spawn with manual stream collection instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Do not manually edit machine-generated artifacts
rules.message: Do not manually edit machine-generated artifacts like compiled-rules.json. Fixes must be applied to the source lessons or the compiler logic to ensure they are not overwritten.

rules.lessonHeading: The Git -- separator treats all subsequent arguments
rules.message: Revision specifiers (like 'branch..HEAD' or 'HEAD') must appear before the '--' separator in Git commands; arguments after '--' are treated strictly as file paths.

rules.lessonHeading: When using the @google/genai SDK (v1+), the constructor
rules.message: When using the @google/genai v1+ SDK, the constructor requires an options object (e.g., { apiKey: '...' }) instead of a raw string or variable.

rules.lessonHeading: Centralize error signature detection (such as 429 status
rules.message: Centralize error signature detection (e.g., 429 status or 'rate limit' strings) into a shared utility to ensure consistent handling across native SDKs and CLI/shell execution paths.

rules.lessonHeading: Use path.relative(process.cwd(),
rules.message: Use path.relative(process.cwd(), path.resolve(process.cwd(), input)) for robust path normalization instead of simple string replacements (e.g. .replace(process.cwd(), '')).

rules.lessonHeading: Always use fully qualified identifiers for caching
rules.message: Always use fully qualified identifiers (e.g., 'provider:model') for cache hashing and telemetry to prevent cross-provider collisions.

rules.lessonHeading: Use vi.importActual in mocks to preserve utility functions
rules.message: Use vi.importActual() or the (importOriginal) factory argument to preserve utility functions. Re-implementing utilities in mocks is brittle.
rules.astQuery: (call_expression function: (member_expression object: (identifier) @vi (#match? @vi "^(vi|vitest)$") property: (property_identifier) @method (#eq? @method "mock")) arguments: (arguments (_) [(arrow_function body: (_) @body (#not-match? @body "importActual|importOriginal")) (function_expression body: (_) @body (#not-match? @body "importActual|importOriginal"))])) @violation

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer Vitest's expect().rejects.toHaveProperty()
rules.message: Prefer Vitest's expect().rejects.toHaveProperty() assertions over manual try/catch blocks with expect.fail()

rules.lessonHeading: Setting open-pull-requests-limit to 0 in dependabot.yml
rules.message: Set open-pull-requests-limit to 0 in dependabot.yml to suppress routine version bumps while allowing security updates via GitHub settings.

rules.lessonHeading: Synchronous file operations are often preferable in CLI
rules.message: Prefer synchronous file operations in CLI tools (e.g., fs.readFileSync) for simplicity; blocking the event loop is acceptable in short-lived CLI processes.

rules.lessonHeading: Provide a dummy fallback API key (e.g., 'local-only')
rules.message: Provide a dummy fallback API key (e.g., 'local-only') instead of an empty string to bypass client-side SDK validation for local endpoints like Ollama.

rules.lessonHeading: Treat usage and token statistics as optional fields
rules.message: Usage and token statistics are optional in many OpenAI-compatible APIs. Access them defensively using optional chaining (e.g., usage?.total_tokens) to prevent runtime crashes when fields are omitted by third-party providers.

rules.lessonHeading: Wrap user-controlled fields like PR descriptions
rules.message: User-controlled fields like PR descriptions or comments must be wrapped in XML tags explicitly labeled as 'untrusted content' within system prompts to prevent injection.

rules.lessonHeading: Casting an object literal to an Error type does not satisfy
rules.message: Casting an object literal to an Error type does not satisfy 'instanceof Error' checks because the prototype chain is missing at runtime. Use 'Object.assign(new Error(message), properties)' to ensure objects pass both TypeScript validation and runtime prototype inspections.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using Zod's .url() validator for configurations where
rules.message: Avoid using Zod's .url() validator for configuration fields; it requires a protocol prefix (like 'http://') which prevents users from providing bare hostnames or host:port combinations.

rules.lessonHeading: Moving security-sensitive regexes (like BiDi stripping
rules.message: Move security-sensitive regexes (BiDi stripping, XML bypass defense) to core packages. Ensure XML patterns allow for optional whitespace (e.g., </\s*tag\s*>) to prevent prompt injection evasion.

rules.lessonHeading: When detecting Bun environments, check for both bun.lockb
rules.message: When detecting Bun environments, check for both 'bun.lockb' and 'bun.lock' (Bun >= 1.2). Ensure detection priority is explicitly defined (e.g., pnpm > yarn > bun > npx).

rules.lessonHeading: Ensure model name validation regexes explicitly allow
rules.message: Model name validation regexes must explicitly allow dots (.) to accommodate newer naming schemes like gpt-5.4.

rules.lessonHeading: Use pnpm exec for workspace binaries in monorepos
rules.message: Use 'pnpm exec' instead of 'pnpm bin' for workspace binaries (more reliable in monorepos)

rules.lessonHeading: Verify CLI availability in shared git hooks before execution
rules.message: Verify CLI availability (e.g., using 'command -v <tool>') before execution in git hooks to prevent brittle CI failures.

rules.lessonHeading: Resolve git root via rev-parse for monorepo compatibility
rules.message: Resolve the git repository root using 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' instead of checking for a local '.git' directory, which is incompatible with monorepos, submodules, and worktrees.

rules.lessonHeading: Windows requires shell:true for git binary resolution
rules.message: When using execFileSync with 'git', include { shell: true } to ensure the binary is resolved correctly on Windows.

rules.lessonHeading: LLMs are notoriously poor at character counting; use
rules.message: LLMs are poor at character counting; use semantic constraints like 'one to two short sentences' instead of numeric character limits to ensure reliable adherence.

rules.lessonHeading: Check for the presence of the 'g' flag before appending it
rules.message: Check for the presence of the 'g' flag before appending it to a new RegExp's flags; re-adding it when already present causes a runtime SyntaxError.

rules.lessonHeading: Use semantic constraints like "one to two short sentences"
rules.message: Use semantic constraints (e.g., 'one to two short sentences') instead of numeric character limits for LLM prompts to ensure reliable adherence to length requirements.

rules.lessonHeading: Check for the presence of the global ('g') flag
rules.message: When dynamically constructing a RegExp from an existing one, check for the presence of the global ('g') flag before appending it. Re-adding 'g' to a pattern that already has it can cause a SyntaxError in some environments.

rules.lessonHeading: Identify larger or more specific text markers (like triple
rules.message: Identify larger or more specific text markers (like triple backticks) before smaller ones (single backticks) during range collection to ensure correct block isolation.

rules.lessonHeading: When implementing custom glob matching for dir/\*/.ext,
rules.message: When implementing custom glob matching for 'dir/**/*.ext', ensure the index check for the double-wildcard separator excludes the start of the string (index 0). This prevents repo-relative paths from being misinterpreted as root-anchored.

rules.lessonHeading: In core packages, a small, tested custom implementation
rules.message: Avoid adding 'micromatch' as a dependency in core packages. Use a small, tested custom implementation for specific pattern shapes to keep the dependency graph lean.

rules.lessonHeading: Use occurrence counts of opening and closing markers
rules.message: Use occurrence counts of opening/closing markers per line instead of simple string inclusion checks to accurately detect corruption in lines with multiple tags.

rules.lessonHeading: Use the --recurse-submodules flag with git ls-files
rules.message: Always use --recurse-submodules with git ls-files to ensure submodule files are included in scans.

rules.lessonHeading: Changesets interactive CLI (pnpm changeset) crashes when
rules.message: The interactive 'pnpm changeset' command crashes in non-TTY/automated environments like CI. For automated releases, write changeset files manually to .changeset/ or use non-interactive subcommands (e.g., 'version').

rules.lessonHeading: MCP session lifecycle limitation
rules.message: The MCP spec does not support session lifecycle hooks (session.start, session.end, or beforeDisconnect). Auto-handoff via these events is not possible.

rules.lessonHeading: ESM intra-module mock requires re-binding
rules.message: Using '...await vi.importActual()' in ESM mocks retains closure references to original functions. Internal calls within the mocked module will not use your mocks unless re-bound.

rules.lessonHeading: When manually parsing CLI arguments, verify that a flag's
rules.message: Manual CLI argument parsing via indexOf + 1 is error-prone. Verify that the flag's value exists and does not start with a hyphen to avoid misinterpreting the next flag as a parameter.

rules.lessonHeading: Provide a dummy fallback API key (e.g., 'local-only')
rules.message: Provide a dummy fallback API key (e.g., 'local-only')

rules.lessonHeading: Hooks designed to block agent actions, such as shield gates
rules.message: Hooks designed to block agent actions (like shield gates) must use synchronous execution (e.g., execSync) to prevent the agent from proceeding before the check completes. Asynchronous patterns can cause race conditions.

rules.lessonHeading: Proactively remove references to planned features from user
rules.message: Remove references to planned features or upcoming capabilities in user guides to avoid misleading users about current functionality.

rules.lessonHeading: The Gemini CLI and Gemini Code Assist (GCA) do not
rules.message: Use 'GEMINI.md' (uppercase) at the project root. Lowercase configuration files like '.gemini/gemini.md' or 'gemini.md' are not recognized by Gemini CLI or Gemini Code Assist.

rules.lessonHeading: Claude Code requires skills to be organized in a nested
rules.message: Claude Code skills must be organized in a nested directory format with a SKILL.md file (e.g., skills/my-skill/SKILL.md) rather than as flat markdown files for the agent to correctly discover them.

rules.lessonHeading: Setting fail-fast: false in matrix workflows ensures
rules.message: Set 'fail-fast: false' in matrix workflows to ensure failures on one OS do not cancel other test runs.

rules.lessonHeading: Anchor command validation patterns with ^ to prevent hooks
rules.message: Anchor command validation patterns with '^' (e.g., /^command/) to prevent hooks from triggering on arguments that contain the command name as a substring.

rules.lessonHeading: When implementing custom glob matching for dir/\*/.ext,
rules.message: When implementing custom glob matching for dir/\*/.ext,

rules.lessonHeading: Use onWarn callbacks in core library modules
rules.message: Core library modules should use an optional onWarn callback instead of console methods directly to avoid hard dependencies on logging frameworks.

rules.lessonHeading: When detecting Bun environments, check for both bun.lockb
rules.message: When detecting Bun environments, check for both bun.lockb

rules.lessonHeading: Centralize error signature detection (such as 429 status
rules.message: Centralize error signature detection (such as 429 status

rules.lessonHeading: CLI command files should use dynamic await import()
rules.message: CLI command files should use dynamic await import() for heavy internal packages instead of top-level static imports. This ensures that operations like --help or version checks remain fast by avoiding unnecessary module loading at startup.

rules.lessonHeading: For local LLM providers, 500 Internal Server Errors often
rules.message: For local LLM providers, 500 Internal Server Errors often

rules.lessonHeading: Use .min(1) on string schemas in config arrays
rules.message: Use .min(1) on string schemas in configuration to reject empty strings at the parsing stage.

rules.lessonHeading: Standard console.log or console.error calls in MCP tools
rules.message: Standard console.log or console.error calls in MCP tools

rules.lessonHeading: Ollama num_ctx and VRAM: The OpenAI-compatible API adapter
rules.message: Ollama num_ctx and VRAM: The OpenAI-compatible API adapter

rules.lessonHeading: Git hooks should utilize fast, deterministic linting tools
rules.message: Git hooks should use fast, deterministic linting tools. AI-powered tools cause non-deterministic delays and friction in the developer workflow.

rules.lessonHeading: Use granular assertions rather than snapshots when testing
rules.message: Use granular assertions rather than snapshots when testing

rules.lessonHeading: ESM intra-module mock requires re-binding
rules.message: ESM intra-module mock requires re-binding

rules.lessonHeading: When reading cache or config files, specifically check
rules.message: When reading cache or config files, specifically check

rules.lessonHeading: Lesson: Lesson headings are strictly limited to 60
rules.message: Lesson: Lesson headings are strictly limited to 60

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer [[ "$VAR" == "pattern"* ]] over piping to grep
rules.message: Prefer shell built-in matching [[ "$VAR" == "pattern"* ]] over piping to grep to avoid subshell performance overhead.

rules.lessonHeading: Standard console.log or console.error calls in MCP tools
rules.message: Standard console calls in MCP tools corrupt the stdio transport protocol. Use a dedicated file-based logger or return formatted system warnings instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Use semantic constraints like "one to two short sentences"
rules.message: Use semantic constraints like "one to two short sentences"

rules.lessonHeading: Drift detector flags lesson file path mentions
rules.message: Drift detector flags lesson file path mentions

rules.lessonHeading: Using a unified "Totem Error" tag in error logs instead
rules.message: Using a unified "Totem Error" tag in error logs instead

rules.lessonHeading: Warning messages triggered by security violations
rules.message: Warning messages triggered by security violations

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid refactoring synchronous factory functions to async
rules.message: Avoid refactoring synchronous factory functions to async

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using exit 0 inside git hooks intended for chaining
rules.message: Avoid using exit 0 inside git hooks intended for chaining

rules.lessonHeading: Dynamic imports intended for performance optimization
rules.message: Dynamic imports should be confined to CLI command entry points. Avoid using them in utility or adapter layers to maintain a clean dependency graph and prevent security scanner flags.

rules.lessonHeading: AI agent memory config architectures differ significantly
rules.message: Incorrect agent configuration file: use 'GEMINI.md' (uppercase) for Gemini CLI as lowercase is not read by default. For Junie, use '.junie/mcp/mcp.json' instead of '.mcp.json'.

rules.lessonHeading: When using the @google/genai SDK (v1+), the constructor
rules.message: When using the @google/genai SDK (v1+), the constructor

rules.lessonHeading: Using String() casting on input patterns can lead to silent
rules.message: Avoid using String() casting on input patterns; this can lead to silent failures by turning objects into '[object Object]'. Use explicit type validation and error handling instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Labeling a prompt as "Regex Rule Extraction" biases models
rules.message: Use neutral terminology like 'Rule Extraction' instead of 'Regex Rule Extraction' to avoid biasing models toward text-based patterns and ensure structural engines (AST/ast-grep) are considered.

rules.lessonHeading: Always use fully qualified identifiers for caching
rules.message: Always use fully qualified identifiers for caching

rules.lessonHeading: In core packages, a small, tested custom implementation
rules.message: In core packages, a small, tested custom implementation

rules.lessonHeading: Hooks designed to block agent actions, such as shield gates
rules.message: Hooks designed to block agent actions, such as shield gates

rules.lessonHeading: Custom database errors should include specific
rules.message: Custom database errors should include a machine-readable code (e.g., 'DATABASE_MISMATCH') as a second argument to enable programmatic recovery and specific UI suggestions.

rules.lessonHeading: Using child.kill() on Windows when shell: true is enabled
rules.message: Using child.kill() on Windows when shell: true is enabled

rules.lessonHeading: Technical documentation must avoid marketing-centric terms
rules.message: Technical documentation must avoid marketing-centric terms like 'guarantees' or 'comprehensive'. Replace absolute claims with specific descriptions of tested environments and observed results.

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer Explicit Metadata Tokens from LLMs Over Heuristics
rules.message: Prefer Explicit Metadata Tokens from LLMs Over Heuristics

rules.lessonHeading: When timing out a child process, do not reject the promise
rules.message: When timing out a child process, do not reject the promise

rules.lessonHeading: Constructing a single regular expression with alternation
rules.message: Use a single regular expression with alternation (e.g., /a|b|c/g) instead of iterating over an array to perform multiple .replace() calls. This is more efficient and scales better.

rules.lessonHeading: Use the --recurse-submodules flag with git ls-files
rules.message: Use the --recurse-submodules flag with git ls-files

rules.lessonHeading: Resolve git root via rev-parse for monorepo compatibility
rules.message: Resolve git root via rev-parse for monorepo compatibility

rules.lessonHeading: Emojis are excluded from all documentation files to adhere
rules.message: Emojis are excluded from documentation files to adhere to professional formatting standards (see CLAUDE.md).

rules.lessonHeading: When intercepting agent tool inputs via hooks, use specific
rules.message: When intercepting agent tool inputs via hooks, use specific

rules.lessonHeading: Orchestrators must dynamically adjust max_tokens based
rules.message: Hardcoded max_tokens detected. Orchestrators must dynamically adjust max_tokens based on the model used (e.g., Haiku vs Opus) to avoid API failures or unnecessary truncation.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid labeling a tool suite as "deterministic" or "fast"
rules.message: Avoid labeling a tool suite as 'deterministic' or 'fast' if it contains AI-powered components. Attribute these properties to specific commands instead (e.g., 'totem lint').

rules.lessonHeading: Verify CLI availability in shared git hooks before execution
rules.message: Verify CLI availability in shared git hooks before execution

rules.lessonHeading: Static analysis tools should read file content using git
rules.message: Static analysis tools should read file content using git

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid inlining tokens or API keys in configuration files
rules.message: Do not inline tokens or API keys in configuration files like .mcp.json or settings.json. Use environment variables instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Include a space or delimiter when concatenating disjoint
rules.message: Include a space or delimiter when concatenating disjoint

rules.message: When injecting vectordb lessons into orchestrator prompts

rules.lessonHeading: Project conventions require a universal 'Totem Error' tag
rules.message: Project conventions require the 'Totem Error' tag for error logs and command-specific tags (e.g., [Init]) instead of generic tags like [Status] or [Log] for status logs.

rules.lessonHeading: AST-based rules often use empty strings for regex patterns,
rules.message: AST-based rules often use empty strings for regex patterns. Always filter by engine type (e.g., rule.engine === 'regex') before creating a RegExp from a rule pattern to prevent matching every line.

rules.lessonHeading: Using split() on markdown headings often misclassifies
rules.message: Avoid using split() on markdown headings as it can misclassify the preamble or lose heading text. Use matchAll to capture heading indices for precise slicing of content blocks.

rules.lessonHeading: Static top-level imports from heavy internal packages delay
rules.message: Static top-level imports from heavy internal packages delay

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid forcing case normalization on file path filters
rules.message: Avoid forcing case normalization on file path filters (e.g., .toLowerCase() or LOWER()) to maintain consistency with case-sensitive filesystems and ensure compatibility with SQL dialects like LanceDB/DataFusion.

rules.lessonHeading: When mapping violations to rules in SARIF generation,
rules.message: When mapping violations to rules in SARIF generation,

rules.lessonHeading: When detecting markers in user-authored files, use
rules.message: When detecting markers in user-authored files, use

rules.lessonHeading: When mapping violations to rules in SARIF generation,
rules.message: When mapping violations to rules in SARIF generation, failing to find a rule index must trigger a hard error instead of falling back to a default index (0).

rules.lessonHeading: Using a boolean flag to track lazy initialization causes
rules.message: Using a boolean flag to track lazy initialization causes

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using dynamic imports in shared utility modules
rules.message: Avoid using dynamic imports in shared utility modules. Dynamic imports are most effective when used inside specific command handlers to defer loading of heavy dependencies.

rules.lessonHeading: When parsing diff --git headers, extract the destination
rules.message: When parsing 'diff --git' headers, handle quoted strings to support filenames with spaces/renames, and prioritize extracting the 'b/' destination path.

rules.lessonHeading: When using advanced git commands like reset to stage demo
rules.message: When using advanced git commands like 'reset' in demos or onboarding scripts, include an inline comment to explain the intent.

rules.lessonHeading: When wrapping external errors or providing fallbacks,
rules.message: Error messages must start with the '[Totem Error]' prefix for consistency and searchability.

rules.lessonHeading: Custom .env parsers must strip CRLF and quotes
rules.message: Custom .env parsers must strip CRLF and quotes

rules.lessonHeading: GCA may suggest reverting dynamic imports back to static
rules.message: GCA may suggest reverting dynamic imports back to static

rules.lessonHeading: Require specific names for failing tests (e.g., "rejects
rules.message: Use specific names for failing tests (e.g., 'rejects empty catch blocks') instead of generic descriptions like 'works correctly'. This ensures the test defines a concrete failure condition.

rules.lessonHeading: Auto-formatters like Prettier can mangle technical metadata
rules.message: Found escaped underscores (\_) or asterisks (\*) in Markdown. This is often caused by Prettier mangling technical metadata/globs and will break glob matching logic.

rules.lessonHeading: Specific LLM models, such as Gemini's text-embedding-004,
rules.message: Specific LLM models, such as Gemini's text-embedding-004,

rules.lessonHeading: Loading large Tree-sitter WASM binaries at the module level
rules.message: Avoid loading Tree-sitter WASM binaries at the module level to prevent cold-start penalties. Use lazy loading instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Project conventions require log.error to use a mandatory
rules.message: Project conventions require 'Totem Error' tag for log.error and command-specific tags for other log levels (warn, info, etc.).

rules.message: When identifying directory-based glob patterns (like

rules.lessonHeading: Brace expansion (e.g., \*.{ts,js}) is not universally
rules.message: Brace expansion (e.g., {ts,js}) is not universally supported and can cause silent matching failures. Expand these patterns into an explicit array of separate strings.

rules.lessonHeading: Ensure model name validation regexes explicitly allow
rules.message: Ensure model name validation regexes explicitly allow

rules.lessonHeading: NEVER inline secrets, tokens, or API keys into agent config
rules.message: NEVER inline secrets, tokens, or API keys in config files. Use .env files; environment variables are inherited automatically without explicit 'env' blocks.

rules.message: Lesson headings are strictly limited to 60 characters to serve as efficient SARIF identifiers and minimize token consumption.

rules.lessonHeading: CLI command actions must wrap asynchronous logic
rules.message: CLI command actions must wrap asynchronous logic in a try/catch block that delegates to a centralized error handler to ensure consistent error reporting.

rules.lessonHeading: Lesson headings must be restricted to 60 characters
rules.message: Lesson headings must be restricted to 60 characters or fewer to ensure stable identifiers in SARIF reports.

rules.lessonHeading: The LLM documentation generator consistently hallucinates
rules.message: Issue #515 (Claude Code hooks) was closed as not implemented. References to it are known LLM hallucinations. Use #520 for the automatic enforcement strategy instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Swallowing errors within internal validation checks (like
rules.message: Do not swallow errors in internal validation checks. Propagate them to allow top-level handlers to identify database corruption or 'healable' states and trigger appropriate resets.

rules.lessonHeading: When masking secrets with assignment patterns, use capture
rules.message: When masking secrets, use capture groups (e.g., `/(key=).*/`) and backreferences (e.g., `'$1REDACTED'`) to preserve the key and assignment syntax for debugging purposes.

rules.lessonHeading: Static analysis tools should read file content using git
rules.message: Use 'git show :path' instead of 'fs' methods to read file content for static analysis. This ensures the tool reads the staged index version rather than unstaged local changes.

rules.lessonHeading: File system watchers should monitor for "rename" events
rules.message: File system watchers should monitor for 'rename' events to support atomic saves. Handling only 'change' or 'modified' often misses updates from editors that save to a temporary file and then rename it.

rules.lessonHeading: Binaries like git may fail to resolve correctly when using

rules.lessonHeading: When parsing git diff output, target the b/ destination
rules.message: When parsing git diff output, ensure you target the 'b/' destination path and account for quoted strings/spaces. Avoid simple whitespace-based matches like 'a/\S+' which fail on renames or paths with spaces.

rules.lessonHeading: Ensure all thrown errors, including those for missing
rules.message: Ensure all thrown errors, including those for missing

rules.lessonHeading: Issue numbers are only unique within a single repository;
rules.message: Issue numbers are only unique within a single repository;

rules.lessonHeading: The text-embedding-004 identifier is frequently unavailable
rules.message: The text-embedding-004 identifier is frequently unavailable

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid inlining tokens or API keys in configuration files
rules.message: Avoid inlining tokens or API keys in configuration files

rules.lessonHeading: Moving instructions from the error message string
rules.message: Move instructional text (e.g., 'Please...', 'Try...') from the error message string to a dedicated 'recoveryHint' property to enable consistent CLI formatting.

rules.lessonHeading: Standardized logging utilities should always be used over
rules.message: Use the standardized 'log' utility instead of 'console.error' for consistent output formatting and tagging.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using console methods directly in core library
rules.message: Avoid using console methods directly in core library

rules.lessonHeading: Proactively remove references to planned features from user
rules.pattern: \b(coming soon|planned (feature|for)|future release|roadmap|under development|slated for|later development phases)\b
rules.message: Proactively remove references to planned features from user

rules.lessonHeading: AI tool metadata must treat MCP-related fields as optional
rules.message: MCP-related fields in tool metadata must be optional (e.g., 'mcp?:' in TypeScript or '.optional()' in Zod) to accommodate agents like GitHub Copilot that lack MCP capabilities.

rules.lessonHeading: Using split() on markdown headings often misclassifies
rules.message: Using split() on markdown headings often misclassifies

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid bypassing visibility checks with bracket notation
rules.message: Avoid bypassing visibility checks with bracket notation

rules.lessonHeading: Spying on underlying implementation details
rules.message: Avoid spying on 'console' methods. Target the project's public logging utility instead to ensure tests remain robust against internal changes.

rules.lessonHeading: When deferring a bot optimization suggestion as "premature"
rules.message: When deferring an optimization as 'premature', document the trigger condition (e.g., 'revisit when list exceeds N entries') to avoid silent accumulation of sub-optimal patterns.

rules.lessonHeading: Standard glob matchers often handle brace expansion
rules.message: Standard glob matchers often handle brace expansion (e.g., {ts,tsx}) inconsistently. Manually expand groups into discrete patterns to ensure comprehensive file coverage.

rules.lessonHeading: Do not manually edit machine-generated artifacts
rules.message: Do not manually edit machine-generated artifacts

rules.lessonHeading: Automated gates like pre-commit or pre-push hooks
rules.message: Automated gates like pre-commit or pre-push hooks

rules.lessonHeading: Log optional component failures at the warn level rather
rules.message: Log optional component failures at the 'warn' level rather than using info, debug, or trace to ensure environmental misconfigurations are discoverable.

rules.lessonHeading: Separate fast, pattern-based checks into a "lint" command
rules.message: Follow intent-based naming: use 'lint' for fast pattern-based checks and 'shield' for thorough AI-powered analysis. Avoid naming commands after their implementation (e.g., 'ai-lint' or 'static-check').

rules.lessonHeading: AI agent memory config architectures differ significantly
rules.message: AI agent memory config architectures differ significantly

rules.lessonHeading: Generating IDs from violation properties like file, line,
rules.message: Generating IDs from violation properties like file, line,

rules.lessonHeading: Detect existing hook managers and provide manual guidance
rules.message: Detect existing hook managers and provide manual guidance

rules.lessonHeading: Normalize file paths for SQL LIKE clauses
rules.message: When using file paths in SQL LIKE clauses, normalize backslashes and explicitly escape % and _ characters (e.g. using a helper function) to prevent accidental wildcard matching.

rules.lessonHeading: When catching and re-logging errors that originate
rules.message: When catching and re-logging errors that originate

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid bypassing visibility checks with bracket notation
rules.message: Avoid bypassing visibility checks with bracket notation for private or initialization methods. Use a public static factory method for clean, type-safe instantiation instead.

rules.lessonHeading: When executing child processes via execSync with stdio:
rules.message: When using execSync with stdio: 'inherit', the child process output is already streamed to the terminal. Adding explicit error logs in the catch block results in duplicate output. Consider removing the log or leaving the catch block empty if the error is already handled by the streamed output.

rules.lessonHeading: Wrap user-controlled fields like PR descriptions
rules.message: Wrap user-controlled fields like PR descriptions

rules.lessonHeading: Git diff headers wrap file paths containing spaces
rules.message: Git diff headers wrap file paths containing spaces

rules.lessonHeading: Synchronous file operations are often preferable in CLI
rules.message: Synchronous file operations are often preferable in CLI

rules.lessonHeading: NEVER inline secrets, tokens, or API keys into agent config
rules.message: NEVER inline secrets, tokens, or API keys into agent config

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid hardcoding "magic numbers" or specific counts
rules.message: Avoid hardcoding specific commit offsets (e.g., HEAD~1) when automating file reverts. Use 'git checkout HEAD' to restore the file to its last committed state, which is more robust against project evolution.

rules.lessonHeading: Omit defensive XML escaping for prompt injection
rules.message: Omit defensive XML escaping for prompt injection

rules.lessonHeading: Treat usage and token statistics as optional fields
rules.message: Treat usage and token statistics as optional fields

rules.lessonHeading: When normalizing diverse SDK errors for internal retry
rules.message: When normalizing diverse SDK errors for internal retry

rules.lessonHeading: Using a unified "Totem Error" tag in error logs instead
rules.message: Use the unified 'Totem Error' tag in error logs instead of command-specific identifiers to ensure consistent observability.

rules.lessonHeading: Individual CLI commands should throw standardized errors
rules.message: Individual CLI commands should throw standardized errors instead of logging or exiting locally. This allows a central top-level handler to manage consistent exit codes, error formatting, and process lifecycle.

rules.lessonHeading: Always generate sentinel markers even when the internal
rules.message: Always generate sentinel markers even when the internal

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using console methods directly in core library
rules.message: Avoid using console methods directly in core library modules to maintain I/O separation. Provide an optional onWarn callback or use a formal logger instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Junie requires specific file locations for auto-detection,
rules.message: Junie requires specific file locations for auto-detection,

rules.lessonHeading: Casting an object literal to an Error type does not satisfy
rules.message: Casting an object literal to an Error type does not satisfy

rules.lessonHeading: For CLI tools reading a small number of local files,
rules.message: In CLI tools, prefer synchronous file system APIs (e.g., readFileSync) over asynchronous ones to minimize promise overhead and code complexity.

rules.lessonHeading: Heavy dependencies or sub-command logic should be
rules.message: Sub-command logic should be dynamically imported within the command handler (using await import()) rather than at the top level to ensure fast CLI startup times.

rules.lessonHeading: Perform shell-level existence checks before invoking CLI
rules.message: Perform shell-level existence checks before invoking CLI

rules.lessonHeading: MCP session lifecycle limitation
rules.message: MCP session lifecycle limitation

rules.lessonHeading: Technical documentation should avoid marketing-centric
rules.message: Avoid marketing-centric terms like 'Local Brain' in technical documentation. Use precise technical descriptions instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Changesets interactive CLI (pnpm changeset) crashes when
rules.message: Changesets interactive CLI (pnpm changeset) crashes when
$TOOL_INPUT is untrusted data from the host AI. It must be quoted in shell commands (e.g., "$TOOL_INPUT") to prevent command injection.

rules.lessonHeading: When re-throwing errors in a CLI orchestrator, always
rules.message: When re-throwing errors in the CLI orchestrator, always include the project's standard error prefix '[Totem Error]' in the message.

rules.lessonHeading: For local LLM providers, 500 Internal Server Errors often
rules.message: Local LLM 500 errors often indicate VRAM or context exhaustion. Include guidance for hardware parameters like 'numCtx' or 'VRAM' in the error message to help users resolve resource failures.

rules.lessonHeading: GitHub Actions shell injection via template expressions
rules.message: GitHub Actions shell injection/word-splitting risk: Map github.event or inputs to env variables first to avoid command injection, and always quote shell variables (e.g., "$VAR") to prevent word-splitting.

rules.lessonHeading: GCA may suggest reverting dynamic imports back to static
rules.message: Do not use static top-level imports for @mmnto/totem in CLI command files; use dynamic imports inside command functions to ensure fast CLI startup performance.

rules.lessonHeading: Issue numbers are only unique within a single repository;
rules.message: Issue numbers are only unique within a single repository; use qualified syntax like 'owner/repo#number' to prevent collisions.

rules.lessonHeading: Standardize exception messages with a consistent prefix
rules.message: Exception messages must start with the '[Totem Error]' prefix to help distinguish internal logic failures from external system errors.

rules.lessonHeading: Escape metacharacters in RegExp interpolation
rules.message: Always escape metacharacters when interpolating dynamic strings into a RegExp constructor to prevent regex injection (e.g., using escape-string-regexp).

rules.lessonHeading: Using empty catch blocks during regex compilation in audit
rules.message: Empty catch blocks in audit scripts hide regex compilation failures. Ensure errors are surfaced to prevent silent coverage gaps and maintain rule-set integrity.

rules.lessonHeading: Technical documentation must replace absolute claims
rules.message: Technical documentation must replace absolute claims like 'guarantee' or 'comprehensive' with specific, evidence-backed descriptions of tested behavior.

rules.lessonHeading: Static top-level imports in CLI command files increase
rules.message: Static top-level imports in CLI command files increase startup latency. Use dynamic import() within the command handler to ensure heavy modules are only loaded when executed.

rules.lessonHeading: Move large imports, such as multi-line prompt templates,
rules.message: Move large multi-line prompt templates inside function bodies to prevent eager parsing at module load time and improve CLI responsiveness.

rules.lessonHeading: Exact-match regexes for HTML tags fail on valid variants
rules.message: Exact-match HTML tag regexes are fragile. Use resilient patterns that handle attributes and whitespace (e.g., /<tag(?:\s+[^>]*)?>/i) to prevent extraction failures on valid variants.

rules.lessonHeading: Always propagate parse warning callbacks in AST-based test
rules.message: Always propagate parse warning callbacks in AST-based tests. Swallowing these warnings via empty functions (e.g., `onWarning: () => {}`) makes it impossible to distinguish between a legitimate 'no match' and a silent syntax error in a test fixture.

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer Vitest's expect().rejects.toHaveProperty()
rules.message: Prefer Vitest's expect().rejects.toHaveProperty()

rules.lessonHeading: Core library modules should accept optional warning
rules.message: Warning callbacks should be optional (e.g. 'warn?:' or as part of an options object) rather than required positional arguments to maintain API flexibility.

rules.lessonHeading: Review metadata from third-party integrations
rules.message: Third-party integration metadata can contain null 'author' fields. Use optional chaining (author?.includes) or an existence check before calling .includes() to prevent runtime crashes.

rules.lessonHeading: The shell option in spawn() calls should be omitted
rules.message: The shell option in spawn() calls should be omitted

rules.lessonHeading: PID-based process liveness checks (process.kill(pid, 0))
rules.message: PID-based process liveness checks (process.kill(pid, 0))

rules.lessonHeading: Check for the presence of the 'g' flag before appending it
rules.message: Check for the presence of the 'g' flag before appending it

rules.lessonHeading: Use path.relative(process.cwd(),
rules.message: Use path.relative(process.cwd(),

rules.lessonHeading: Perform explicit null and type checks on the results
rules.message: Perform explicit null and type checks on the results

rules.lessonHeading: Empty catch blocks during file discovery can lead
rules.message: Empty catch blocks can lead to incomplete data sets and incorrect logic. Use explicit error handling to distinguish between missing directories and file read failures.

rules.lessonHeading: When manually parsing CLI arguments, verify that a flag's
rules.message: When manually parsing CLI arguments, verify that a flag's

rules.lessonHeading: The @ast-grep/napi findAll method accepts both string
rules.message: The @ast-grep/napi findAll method accepts both string

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid naive substring matching when extracting counts
rules.message: Avoid naive substring matching for 'lessonHash'. Anchor the search pattern to the object property (e.g., /lessonHash\\s*:/) to prevent false positives in comments, imports, or types.

rules.lessonHeading: When scanning directories for feature detection
rules.message: Do not swallow all errors in an empty catch block. When scanning directories for feature detection, explicitly handle known errors (like ENOENT) and log or rethrow others (like EACCES) to avoid masking structural or permission issues.

rules.lessonHeading: Use anchored regex for shell command matching
rules.message: Use anchored regex (e.g., /^\s*git\s+/) for shell command matching instead of loose keyword matching to prevent accidental matches on file paths (like .git/hooks) or comments.

rules.lessonHeading: Always use dynamic imports for heavy logic or detection
rules.message: Always use dynamic imports (await import(...)) for heavy logic or detection modules within CLI command functions to ensure fast startup performance.

rules.lessonHeading: Query engines must throw exceptions on parsing or query
rules.message: Query engines must throw exceptions on parsing or query crashes instead of returning empty result sets to prevent fail-open security vulnerabilities.

rules.lessonHeading: When determining if a session is interactive, check both
rules.message: When determining if a session is interactive, check both stdin.isTTY and stdout.isTTY. This prevents the CLI from emitting ANSI escape codes or interactive prompts when output is being piped to another process or redirected to a file.

rules.lessonHeading: Functions designed to always throw an exception
rules.message: Functions designed to always throw should explicitly return the 'never' type to inform TypeScript's control flow analysis and eliminate redundant return statements at call sites.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid relying on the test runner's environment to determine
rules.message: Avoid relying on process.stdout.isTTY directly. Use an injectable option to override TTY detection so that non-interactive error paths can be tested deterministically.

rules.lessonHeading: Use strict boundaries (like (\/|$)) or the URL class
rules.message: Insecure prefix matching for local providers. Use strict boundaries (like '(\/|$)') or the URL class to prevent bypasses (e.g., 'localhost.evil.com' matching a 'localhost' prefix check).

rules.lessonHeading: Using require() inside vi.mock callbacks can lead
rules.message: Using require() inside vi.mock callbacks can lead to resolution failures in pure ESM environments. Use vi.importActual or async imports instead.

rules.lessonHeading: git ls-files with relative paths fails from subdirs
rules.message: Use 'git -C "$REPO_ROOT"' or absolute paths with 'git ls-files' to ensure the command works correctly from any subdirectory.

rules.lessonHeading: Standard string length checks like .min(1) fail to catch
rules.message: Standard Zod string length checks fail on whitespace-only input. Apply .trim() before .min(), .length(), or .nonempty() to ensure meaningful content.

rules.lessonHeading: Regex patterns designed to protect code blocks must include
rules.message: Regex patterns or strings identifying code blocks must include tilde fences (~~~) alongside backticks (```) to avoid missing valid markdown code blocks.

rules.lessonHeading: Use fixed literal tags for log.error calls
rules.message: Use the fixed literal tag 'Totem Error' in log.error calls instead of variables or other strings to ensure consistent CLI error reporting.

rules.lessonHeading: When dynamically generating XML fragments, the tag name
rules.message: Dynamic XML tag names must be validated against a strict regex to prevent injection. Escaping content alone is insufficient if the surrounding tag can be manipulated.

rules.lessonHeading: Hash dirs with git ls-files for cross-platform stability
rules.message: Use 'git ls-files -s <dir> | git hash-object --stdin' instead of standard hashing tools for directory contents to ensure cross-platform CRLF/LF stability.

rules.lessonHeading: Named constants for file permission literals
rules.message: Use named constants for file permissions instead of inline magic numbers (e.g., 0o644) to improve readability and security auditing.

rules.lessonHeading: Implementing bypass logic via GitHub Actions contains()
rules.message: Use GitHub Actions `contains()` in an `if:` condition instead of shell-based parsing (like grep or [[ ]]) of PR/issue metadata to prevent shell injection.

rules.lessonHeading: Preserve error context when re-throwing
rules.message: Preserve the original error context when re-throwing (e.g., use 'throw new Error(`...: ${err.message}`)' or 'throw new Error(message, { cause: err })') to avoid swallowing debug details.

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer shell redirection over cat pipe
rules.message: Prefer shell redirection (command < file) over piping cat (cat file | command) to avoid unnecessary processes.

rules.lessonHeading: Background processes spawned by Git hooks should redirect
rules.message: Background processes spawned by Git hooks must redirect stdout and stderr (e.g., '> log 2>&1 &') to prevent terminal pollution.

rules.lessonHeading: Adding 'edited' to pull_request trigger types ensures
rules.message: GitHub Action pull_request triggers should include the 'edited' type to ensure workflows rerun when the PR title changes (e.g., to detect bypass strings like [UPDATE-RULES]).

rules.lessonHeading: MCP tools should return plain-text for error paths
rules.message: MCP tools should return plain-text for error paths. Do not use XML wrapping (like formatXmlResponse) when isError is true to ensure client compatibility.

rules.lessonHeading: Implementing a trap for EXIT, INT, and TERM signals ensures
rules.message: When implementing a cleanup trap, ensure you catch EXIT, INT, and TERM signals together to reliably handle interruptions and temporary file cleanup.

rules.lessonHeading: When redacting tokens like sk-proj- and sk-, the longest
rules.message: Longest patterns must be evaluated first during redaction. Ensure 'sk-proj-' is handled before 'sk-' to prevent partial matching and sensitive data exposure.

rules.lessonHeading: Map PR metadata to env vars for secure shells
rules.message: Map PR metadata to environment variables instead of using 'gh api' to prevent shell injection and avoid unnecessary 'pull-requests: read' permissions.

rules.lessonHeading: Use dynamic await import() inside CLI command handlers
rules.message: Use dynamic await import() inside CLI command handlers for internal packages instead of top-level static imports to ensure fast startup performance.

rules.lessonHeading: Relying on error name strings for matching is fragile
rules.message: Relying on error name strings for matching is fragile. Use 'instanceof' or specific error codes for robust, type-safe error handling.

rules.lessonHeading: Core library modules should use optional callbacks
rules.message: Use optional callbacks (e.g., onWarn) instead of hardcoded console methods in core library modules to remain environment-agnostic.

rules.lessonHeading: Sanitize untrusted PR content by escaping closing tags
rules.message: Sanitize untrusted PR content by escaping closing tags (e.g., .replace(/<\//g, '<\\/')) to prevent attackers from breaking out of XML envelopes and injecting malicious instructions.

rules.lessonHeading: Using input strings as Map keys in batch processing
rules.message: Using input strings as Map keys in batch processing causes data loss when duplicate inputs are provided. Returning an array of results mapped to input indices ensures every call is uniquely handled and preserved.

rules.lessonHeading: Reject non-positive PIDs and infinite timestamps
rules.message: Reject non-positive PIDs and infinite timestamps in lockfiles to prevent accidental signaling of process groups (via kill(0)) and broken staleness math.

rules.lessonHeading: Simple string lookups like indexOf('[{') fail to detect
rules.message: Simple string lookups like indexOf('[{') fail to detect pretty-printed JSON; use a regex search for /\[\s*{/ instead to correctly identify the start of an array regardless of whitespace.

rules.lessonHeading: Replacing direct process.exit(1) calls with thrown custom
rules.message: Avoid direct process.exit() calls; throw custom errors instead to keep logic composable and centralize exit handling at the application boundary.

rules.lessonHeading: Using parseInt() allows malformed strings like '5foo' to be
rules.message: Use Number() or explicit validation instead of parseInt() to prevent parsing malformed strings (e.g., '5foo')

rules.lessonHeading: Confine dynamic imports to CLI command handlers to maintain
rules.message: Dynamic imports must be confined to CLI command handlers to maintain a clean dependency graph and pass security scans.

rules.lessonHeading: The github.event.pull_request context is immutable
rules.message: The github.event.pull_request context is immutable for a specific workflow run. Manual re-runs will not reflect title or body edits made after the initial trigger. Fetch the latest PR data via the GitHub API if current values are required.

rules.lessonHeading: In the DataFusion SQL dialect, backslashes are treated
rules.message: In DataFusion SQL, backslashes are treated as literal characters. Use doubled single quotes ('') instead of '\'' to escape single quotes within string literals.

rules.lessonHeading: Calling .test() on a global regex increments the lastIndex
rules.message: Calling .test() on a global regex increments lastIndex, which can cause subsequent replacements or tests to skip characters. Use a non-global regex instead.

rules.lessonHeading: When testing expected failures, assert the specific error
rules.message: When testing expected failures, assert the specific error message or violation count instead of just checking if an error was thrown to prevent false positives.

rules.lessonHeading: Instead of re-running a test suite to show failure logs,
rules.message: Instead of re-running the test suite to show logs, capture output to a temporary file during the first run and display it on failure to avoid doubling execution time.

rules.lessonHeading: BSD sed on macOS does not support the \x1b hex escape; use
rules.message: BSD sed on macOS does not support \x1b; use perl or col -b to strip ANSI codes.

rules.lessonHeading: When using stdio: 'pipe' with the GitHub CLI, set
rules.message: When using stdio: 'pipe' with the GitHub CLI, set GH_PROMPT_DISABLED=1 to prevent indefinite hangs.

rules.lessonHeading: Manual lessons must use the '## Lesson — <Heading>'
rules.message: Manual lessons must use the '## Lesson — <Heading>' structure and include a 'Tags' field.

rules.lessonHeading: Hardcoded vendor-shaped secrets in test fixtures
rules.message: Hardcoded vendor-shaped secrets in test fixtures can trigger false positives. Construct these strings at runtime via concatenation (e.g., 'sk_' + 'live_...') to avoid detection.

rules.lessonHeading: Using 'pnpm run version' instead of the bare 'pnpm version'
rules.message: Use 'pnpm run version' instead of 'pnpm version' to ensure Changesets workflow triggers correctly and updates changelogs.

rules.lessonHeading: Use a '## Lesson — <Heading>' format and 'Tags' field
rules.message: Lesson files must use the '## Lesson — <Heading>' format (with an em-dash) and a 'Tags:' field (capitalized, plural).

rules.lessonHeading: Instead of hardcoding .git/hooks, use 'git rev-parse
rules.message: Avoid hardcoding '.git/hooks'. Use 'git rev-parse --git-path hooks' to support custom hook paths and worktrees correctly.

rules.lessonHeading: Using runtime binary resolution for hooks instead
rules.message: Use runtime binary resolution instead of hardcoded absolute paths for binaries (e.g., use 'python3' or '/usr/bin/env python3' instead of '/usr/bin/python3') to ensure language-agnostic support.

rules.lessonHeading: Store the actual resolved vector size in the registry
rules.message: Store the actual resolved vector size in the registry instead of 'auto' placeholders to provide accurate diagnostic information about the embedding configuration.

rules.lessonHeading: Set LLM temperature to zero during build
rules.message: LLM temperature must be set to 0 during build/compilation to ensure deterministic output and stable manifest hashes.

rules.lessonHeading: Use a Personal Access Token (PAT) for automated PR creation
rules.message: Use a Personal Access Token (PAT) instead of GITHUB_TOKEN for automated PR creation if organization restrictions are in place.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid hardcoding .git/hooks paths as repositories may use
rules.message: Avoid hardcoding '.git/hooks'; use 'git rev-parse --git-path hooks' to account for custom core.hooksPath configurations.

rules.lessonHeading: Swallowing read errors during security scans can lead
rules.message: Swallowing read errors during security scans can lead to false passes. Explicitly record failure states if files are unreadable.

rules.lessonHeading: Creating changeset Markdown files manually
rules.message: Create changeset Markdown files manually in .changeset/ instead of using the interactive CLI to avoid stdin piping failures.

rules.lessonHeading: Hardcoding vendor-shaped secret literals in test fixtures
rules.message: Avoid hardcoding vendor-shaped secret literals in test fixtures; construct these strings dynamically at runtime to avoid triggering security scanners.

rules.lessonHeading: Explicitly capture stderr when a tool probes multiple
rules.message: Explicitly capture or redirect stderr (e.g., 2>/dev/null) when probing repositories with gh/git to prevent terminal pollution from 'not found' or GraphQL errors.

rules.lessonHeading: CWD-relative writes cause monorepo chaos
rules.message: Do not use process.cwd() to resolve paths; use the project root from the config loader to prevent orphaned artifacts in monorepo subdirectories.

rules.lessonHeading: Library code should use an onWarn callback instead
rules.message: Use an onWarn callback instead of hardcoding console.warn in library code to allow consumers to route or suppress output.

rules.lessonHeading: When testing diagnostic output, assert that warnings
rules.message: When testing diagnostic output, assert that warnings include the specific file path prefix (e.g., 'path/to/file: warning') to ensure correct attribution.

rules.lessonHeading: Frontmatter delimiters must be anchored to the start
rules.message: Frontmatter delimiters must be anchored to the start of the line (no leading whitespace) to prevent misinterpretation.

rules.lessonHeading: Resolve persistent directories like caches relative
rules.message: Resolve persistent directories (like caches) relative to the configuration file's location rather than process.cwd() to prevent orphaned directories when executed from different subdirectories.

rules.lessonHeading: Dynamic imports used for CLI startup performance should be
rules.message: Dynamic imports should be restricted to command entry points to maintain a clean dependency graph and pass security scans in utility layers.

rules.lessonHeading: Using a unified 'Totem Error' tag across all CLI commands
rules.message: CLI error logs must include the 'Totem Error' tag for consistent filtering (e.g., console.error('Totem Error: <message>'))

rules.lessonHeading: Normalizing shallow patterns like .ts to /.ts ensures
rules.message: Normalize shallow patterns like '*.ts' to '**/*.ts' to ensure consistent recursive behavior across tools like SARIF and picomatch.

rules.lessonHeading: When applying global error prefixes, check for existing
rules.message: Check for existing '[Error]' tags (e.g., using .startsWith()) before prepending to avoid redundant prefixes like '[Error] [Error] message'.

rules.lessonHeading: Silent failures in 'best-effort' operations like staleness
rules.message: Use non-blocking logging instead of empty catch blocks to ensure IO failures are traceable without crashing the primary command execution.

rules.lessonHeading: Differentiating between */.ext and dir/*.ext in LLM prompts
rules.message: Use recursive glob patterns (e.g., '**/*.ts') or directory-scoped patterns (e.g., 'dir/*.ts') instead of shallow patterns (e.g., '*.ts') to ensure portability across environments.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid broad negative tokens like \bno\b when detecting
rules.message: Avoid broad negative tokens like '\bno\b' when detecting pushback to prevent false positives on phrases like 'No worries'.

rules.lessonHeading: Manually joining strings for YAML frontmatter can break
rules.message: Avoid manual string joining for YAML frontmatter. Use a proper serializer (like js-yaml) to handle special characters and escaping.

rules.lessonHeading: Constructing shell commands with string interpolation
rules.message: Avoid string interpolation in shell commands. Use argument arrays with spawnSync to prevent command injection and handle spaces/metacharacters safely.

rules.lessonHeading: Reporting malformed records via an onWarn callback instead
rules.message: Avoid silent catch blocks. Use an onWarn callback to report malformed records or corrupted NDJSON streams to allow debugging without interrupting execution.

rules.lessonHeading: Updating comment-matching regex to support trailing inline
rules.message: Comment-matching regex should not be anchored to the start of the line (^) to support trailing inline directives.
